key_insight: A multi-task spatiotemporal deep neural network can predict laser welding penetration state, depth, and seam morphology from weld pool images and process parameters in a single framework.
engineering_relevance: The approach enables in-situ quality control for laser penetration welding by improving real-time defect detection and weld quality assessment.
method: Uses CMOS camera images of the top weld pool during welding.
Combines convolutional neural networks with state-space models to extract spatial-temporal features.
Fuses weld pool imagery with welding parameters in a multi-task prediction model.
evidence_signal: On the test set, the model achieved 99.35% penetration-state accuracy, 1.79 mm penetration-depth error, and 95.65% cross-section reconstruction accuracy.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the quality and representativeness of the constructed dataset, so robustness under new materials or welding conditions should be verified.
paper_title: A multi-task spatiotemporal deep neural network for predicting penetration depth and morphology in laser welding
